notes: Eval 1 (single table): Both with/without skill scored 100% — the skill adds structure and cleaner output but both found the same core information. The key differentiator is efficiency: skill used 36 tool calls in 267s vs baseline's 103 tool calls in 637s.

Eval 2 (multi-table SQL): Biggest skill win — 100% vs 33%. Baseline found the naming issue but missed PII warnings, cross-table synthesis, team/channel context, and the Flags & Warnings section entirely.
Eval 3 (schema owner): Baseline actually won (100% vs 80%) because its Slack token was live while the with-skill run had an expired token. This is an environment issue, not a skill quality issue.
Slack token expiry is a recurring obstacle — affected the with-skill agent for eval 3. The xoxp token in mcp-credentials.md needs refreshing.
Without-skill baseline shows high variance (stddev 0.31) — it works well on simple factual lookups but falls apart on multi-step structured analysis (eval 2). The skill provides consistent structure.
Time efficiency: skill is 90s faster on average. The baseline for eval 1 burned 103 tool calls thrashing around without a plan — the skill's structured steps prevent this.

Both approaches rely heavily on Notion as the primary documentation source. Snowflake connector (pip install snowflake-connector-python) and Slack token refresh are the highest-priority fixes to unlock full capability.
